classroomLaunch.label: Open Court Getting Started
notes: Use Open Court Getting Started rather than Unit 1.
Focus on routines, discussion rules, listening expectations, foundational skills, and classroom launch.
Open Court writing is not used.

curriculumStart.readingTitle: The Mice Who Lived in a Shoe

morning-work.objective: Students enter, unpack, begin the posted task, and practice independent work expectations.
materials: Morning work page or board task
teacherSteps: Prompt students to unpack and begin immediately.
Take attendance and complete essential morning checks.
Review the task briefly if needed.
morning-work.studentTask: Complete the morning task quietly and prepare materials for MOWR.

mowr.objective: Students practice decoding, fluency, vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension through targeted rotations.
teacherSteps: Prioritize Red daily, Yellow 3-4 times weekly, Blue 2 times weekly, and Green 1-2 times weekly.
mowr.studentTask: Rotate through assigned stations and complete the group task for today.

heggerty.objective: Students build phonemic awareness through oral blending, segmenting, deleting, and substituting sounds.
teacherSteps: Keep the lesson oral and fast paced.
Correct immediately and have students repeat the correct response.
heggerty.studentTask: Respond orally and use the taught hand motions.

phonics.objective: Students connect sounds to spellings and apply the pattern in blending, decoding, dictation, and spelling.
teacherSteps: Assign the aligned Skill Practice page when appropriate.
phonics.studentTask: Read, blend, spell, and write words with the target pattern.

vocabulary.objective: Students learn and use the lesson vocabulary in speaking, reading, and discussion.
teacherSteps: Say the word and have students repeat it.
Invite students to generate an example.
vocabulary.studentTask: Repeat, define, discuss, and apply the vocabulary words.

reading.objective: Students listen, read, discuss, and respond using the lesson comprehension focus and essential question.
teacherSteps: Build background and preview the genre.
Read the selection using the Teacher Edition prompts.
Pause for comprehension checks and discussion starters.
Use the Open Court science connection when included.
Do not use the Open Court writing lesson.
reading.studentTask: Listen, read, discuss, cite details, and complete the aligned response task.
